modes.name: sgov-only
modes.notes: Precious-metals candidates removed from the candidate set; SGOV remains the fallback policy asset.

modes.name: sgov-plus-metals
modes.notes: Precious-metals candidates included, with max one precious holding and GLTR overlap blocking.

scenario_expectations.name: weakest-held-below-floor-to-sgov
scenario_expectations.calibration_note: Defaults preserve SGOV fallback when no candidate clears both floor and delta.

scenario_expectations.name: weakest-held-below-floor-to-gltr
scenario_expectations.calibration_note: Defaults allow a clearly superior precious basket to replace SGOV fallback.

scenario_expectations.name: precious-to-sgov
scenario_expectations.calibration_note: Defaults still allow risk-off parking when precious alternatives are only marginally better.

scenario_expectations.name: sgov-to-metal
scenario_expectations.calibration_note: Defaults let SGOV exit into metal only when the improvement is decisive.

scenario_expectations.name: second-precious-holding-blocked
scenario_expectations.calibration_note: Constraint behavior dominates threshold tuning here.

scenario_expectations.name: candidate-fails-delta
scenario_expectations.calibration_note: Defaults keep marginal candidates from churning the portfolio.

justification: min_floor=0.55 keeps weak absolute candidates out of the portfolio
with precious enabled, strong metals can still beat SGOV fallback
constraint-driven cases remain stable under the same defaults
recommendation.follow_up_notes: Add another tuning issue only if broader real-world scenario packs show SGOV fallback is too sticky or metals are too eager to win.
